revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二荒山神社]]
'''Futarasan Shrine''' (二荒山神社, ふたあらやまじんじゃ, also referred to as Futarasan Jinja or Nikko Jinja) is a Shinto shrine bearing the name "Futara."
== Overview ==
It is associated with the deity of '''Futara''' from the former {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|qid=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}).
Futarasan Shrines either honor the Futara deity or were established as branch shrines of the main {{ill|Futarasan shrine|lt=Futarasan shrine|lt=Futarasan Shrine|ja|二荒山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futarasan Shrine|qq=}}. According to the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, the province had a [[Myōjin Taisha|myōjin taisha]] shrine known as "Futarasan Shrine" in {{ill|Kawachi_District,_Tochigi|en|Kawachi_District,_Tochigi|qid=Q44611|lt=Kawachi District, Tochigi|1=Kawachi District, Tochigi}}. The [[Rikkokushi]] chronicles state that the deity, known as Futara no Kami, was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank Lower in 836 (Shōwa 3) and further elevated to Senior Second Rank in 869 (Jōgan 11).
== Etymology of "Futara" ==
Historically, "Futara" was read as "Nikō," which later evolved into the name {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|qid=Q235753|lt=Nikkō|1=Nikkō}}. Earlier records, such as the {{ill|Kujo Family Manuscript of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|九条家本延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujo Family Manuscript of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, include the reading "Futara no" for Futarasan Shrine, suggesting its pronunciation as "Futara" or "Futaara."<ref name="kanno">Tomokazu Kanno, "A Study of Nikkō and Futarasan – Focused on Etymology," *Konan Women’s University Research Bulletin, Literature and Culture Edition 40*, March 2004.</ref>
In earlier periods, "Futara" referred to the mountain name "Futara no Yama" ("Two Rough Mountains"), symbolizing {{ill|Mount Nantai|en|Mount Nantai|qid=Q1964765|lt=Mount Nantai|1=Mount Nantai}} and {{ill|Mount Nyohō|en|Mount Nyohō|qid=Q11446413|lt=Mount Nyohō|1=Mount Nyohō}}. These twin peaks, seen as a male and female pair akin to {{ill|Mount Tsukuba|en|Mount Tsukuba|qid=Q1754619|lt=Mount Tsukuba|1=Mount Tsukuba}}, represent divine unity.<ref name="kanno" />
The "Ara" part of the name implies reverence for divine power, meaning "a mountain inhabited by a powerful spirit."<ref name="kanno" />
== Myōjin Taisha: Futarasan Shrine ==
The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists "Shimotsuke Province, Kawachi District, Futarasan Shrine" as a myōjin taisha. Two shrines claim this designation: * '''{{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|qid=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine}}''' (宇都宮二荒山神社, located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|qid=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture) * '''{{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|qid=Q701927|lt=Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|1=Futarasan shrine}}''' (日光二荒山神社, located in {{ill|Nikkō|en|Nikkō|qid=Q235753|lt=Nikkō|1=Nikkō}}, Tochigi Prefecture)
Although they differ in deities and origins, both shrines are connected to the {{ill|Shimotsuke clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}.
=== Shrine Disputes ===
During the Meiji period, disputes arose regarding the historical designation of the myōjin taisha. In 1871, {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|qid=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine}} was listed as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-chūsha|ja|官幣中社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-chūsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (imperial shrine of medium Rank). However, {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|qid=Q701927|lt=Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|1=Futarasan shrine}} was later recognized as the designated shrine, resulting in the Utsunomiya shrine's demotion to {{ill|kensha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kensha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} (prefectural shrine). In 1883 (Meiji 16), the Utsunomiya shrine regained recognition as a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} and returned to its previous status.
Currently, both shrines claim the title of myōjin taisha and {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} of Shimotsuke Province.
=== Shrine Rankings ===
The following Ranks of Futara no Kami are recorded in the [[Rikkokushi]]: * 836 (Shōwa 3): Promoted to [[Senior Fifth Rank]] Lower ({{ill|Shōsen'i|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsen'i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}}). * 869 (Jōgan 11): Attained [[Senior Second Rank]].
== List of Shrines ==
There are 30 shrines registered as Futarasan Shrines, 18 as Futarajin Shrines, and 3 as Futarasha Shrines.
=== Main Shrines === * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|qid=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Utsunomiya City) ☆ * {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|qid=Q701927|lt=Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|1=Futarasan shrine}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Nikkō City) ☆
=== Tochigi Prefecture === * {{ill|Futarasan Shrine (Utsunomiya City)|ja|二荒山神社 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futarasan Shrine (Utsunomiya City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504195|1=Futarasan Shrine (Utsunomiya City)}} ☆ * {{ill|Futarasan Shrine (Nikkō City)|ja|二荒山神社 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futarasan Shrine (Nikkō City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504197|1=Futarasan Shrine (Nikkō City)}} ☆
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/260201.html]
